rationale: Both correctly trace the guard to #4834 (matching yenuo26's comment) but then confidently assert the test was 'already fixed on main' with specific line numbers, diffs, and test names (e.g. test_level2_sleep_wake_raises) that appear nowhere in the actual thread — which instead shows Flink-ddd still asking to trigger a full CI sleep-mode run, i.e. unresolved, not closed. Y states this fabricated resolution with zero hedging and piles on more invented specifics (extra line ranges, a companion RuntimeError guard); X at least appends an explicit caveat that the merge-state claims are unverified, which is more honest given the genuine uncertainty in the ground truth. Neither should have declared 'close'/'no further action needed' as confidently as they did.
